meaning: adj.河边的,水滨的n.河岸拥有人
examples: That is fraught with difficulties as there is no seabed agreement between the riparian states.
Habitat: riparian. This plant grows at the sunlit margins of moist, humid riparian woodlands, where it climbs trees and other plants.
Most of the sediment directly enters the headwaters of the Mekong River or is deposited in riparian areas where it is entrained during later floods.
In places like southern New Zealand, intensification of farming has had significant impacts on riparian environments.
Ranged against the project is a coalition of riparian states, pro-poor and environmental activists like the bishop and a battery of dissenting experts.
